Patch Set 2:

Hi Andreas and Sébastien,

This patch concludes the new features in the multi-fileset for now. It is another QoL update for administrating filesets so that a fileset does not need to be removed and re-added if changes are necessary. Instead, this new "lctl nodemap_fileset_modify" command can be used for renaming the fileset, changing the fileset type, and modifying the mount permissions (rw vs ro) in-place.

Can you please review it?
Topic set to mtfl_p2

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

(8 comments)

-1 because of jt_nodemap_fileset_modify() option ordering, but seems reasonable otherwise.

I am a bit concerned about the "delete fileset on error" behavior, but there is also a balance between "stringent error handling" and "ease of use", and I don't have as good an idea about the conditions that might lead to a corrupted fileset definition.

Thanks Marc, looks good overall. Just a few questions/comments I have.

First, just to mention that for the upcoming patch that will provide support for dynamic nodemap, the use case presented in the commit message:

lctl nodemap_fileset_modify --name nm1 --fileset "/remote" --rename
"/othersite" --ro --alt

would return an error for dynamic nodemap if the parent has a primary fileset. Indeed, that would leave the dynamic nodemap with no primary fileset, escaping the parent's namespace.

Actually, I am even wondering. In the general case, do we really want to allow converting a primary fileset into an alternate fileset? That leads to "transparently" removing the primary fileset, which could be dangerous?

> Thanks Marc, looks good overall. Just a few questions/comments I have.

Sébastien, many thanks for the review. 

> First, just to mention that for the upcoming patch that will provide support for dynamic nodemap, the use case presented in the commit message:
> 
> lctl nodemap_fileset_modify --name nm1 --fileset "/remote" --rename
> "/othersite" --ro --alt
> 
> would return an error for dynamic nodemap if the parent has a primary fileset. Indeed, that would leave the dynamic nodemap with no primary fileset, escaping the parent's namespace.
> 
> Actually, I am even wondering. In the general case, do we really want to allow converting a primary fileset into an alternate fileset? That leads to "transparently" removing the primary fileset, which could be dangerous?

Yes, correct. The `check_fileset_{add,delete}_*() functions would need be included into the fileset_modify workflow, which should then handle these cases. I don't think it is a problem to include it, but I have to check for the follow-up patch.

Nevertheless, I had a similar thought, and whether it would be preferable to let  nodemap_fileset_modify only support rename and the ro,rw flags on dynamic nodemaps. On the other hand, I would like to avoid these special cases because it would make dynamic nodemaps more complicated to use, adding additional asterisks.

I understand what you mean regarding that transparently removing the fileset could be dangerous from a usage perspective. However, it should be clear, as per the manpage, that it converts/moves the fileset, so it should be ok.

The new patch updated some points on the man page and comments.

commit.subject: LU-18357 ptlrpc: Add in-place modification of filesets
commit.message:
LU-18357 ptlrpc: Add in-place modification of filesets

To modify a nodemap's fileset, i.e., its subdirectory, type, or
access permissions, the fileset needs to be removed and re-added.

This patch adds the "lctl nodemap_fileset_modify" command to modify a
fileset in-place. For example, the command

lctl nodemap_fileset_modify --name nm1 --fileset "/remote" --rename
"/othersite" --ro --alt

renames the existing read-write primary fileset "/remote" to
"/othersite", making it alternate and read-only.

A new sanity-sec test 27f is added test this command.
